##NAMES=Dendoraite-(NH4)
##RRUFFID=R250048
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(NH_4_)_2_NaAl(C_2_O_4_)(PO_3_OH)_2_(H_2_O)_2_
##LOCALITY=Rowley mine, Maricopa County, Arizona, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless blades that occur in bat-guano-related, post-mining assemblage of phases, associated with antipinite, fluorite, mimetite, mottramite, relianceite-(K) (R250049), rowleyite, salammoniac, struvite, vanadinite, willemite, wulfenite and at least one other new mineral. Type sample described in Kampf et al (2022)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250048
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[(NH_4_)_1.48_K_0.52_]Na_0.96_(Al_0.96_Fe^3+^_0.03_)(C_2_O_4_)[PO_2.97_(OH)_1.03_]_2_(H_2_O)_2_ ; (NH_4_)_2_O,  C_2_O_3_ and H_2_O component is based on the structure
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.702 b: 6.289 c: 19.236 alpha: 90 beta: 90.961 gamma: 90 volume: 1294.5 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
